concerns.text: Task T028 ('validate... against CodeXGLUE benchmark (if available) or document limitation') introduces a conditional escape hatch that violates FR-011. The spec mandates the system MUST validate the proxy against a human-labeled benchmark. Allowing the task to simply 'document a limitation' if the benchmark is unavailable constitutes a silent relaxation of the requirement to implement the validation logic or explicitly reject the study design, rather than implementing the fallback.

Task T012 specifies fetching a 'subset (Python/Java)' but lacks the explicit logic to separate these into a training set (Python) and a held-out validation set (Java) as required by FR-009 and SC-004. Without a distinct task to perform this split and run a separate correlation analysis on the held-out set, the requirement to 'validate... on a held-out set' is semantically weakened to a mere stratification in a single plot.

Task T015 mentions 'normalizing loss by n-gram probability' (FR-010) but omits the prerequisite task to 'build or load the n-gram language model (kenlm)' required to generate these probabilities. The plan lists kenlm as a dependency, but the task list fails to include the implementation step to initialize or train this model, creating a semantic gap where the normalization cannot be executed.

Task T013 mentions running 'CodeQL/tree-sitter' generically. FR-002 explicitly requires running CodeQL and tree-sitter to generate specific labels. The task description lacks the explicit definition of the CodeQL CLI execution parameters or the specific queries/rules to be used, risking a semantic drift where the CodeQL component is omitted or implemented incorrectly given its specific CLI requirements mentioned in the plan.

Task T014 implements LLM inference but lacks a specific sub-task or logic to enforce the '60 seconds per chunk' performance constraint mandated by FR-003. Without a task to implement benchmarking, profiling, or timeout enforcement logic, the requirement is unverified and effectively unenforced in the pipeline, constituting a weakening of the performance constraint.
